what_it_does: Defines a v2 mockup fix brief with global design rules, per-mockup corrections, deferred follow-up ideas, and an execution workflow for producing v2 HTML/renders/manifest entries.
observations: The file opens by framing the pass as a mockup update derived from an adversarial panel round, with tokenisation explicitly deferred.

Global rules G1-G10 specify shared visual constraints for blueprint texture, footer hatch bands, canvas fill, title treatment, charcoal title ink, warm-metal accent roles, surface colors, shadow depth, plate headings, and italic emphasis.
Per-mockup fixes cover var-1 through var-16 plus test-A/test-B/test-C, combining shared G* application with local corrections.
Several fixes prohibit specific visual readings such as blue cast, lemon-yellow hatch, gold fills, beige wash, grey monospace tables, self-narrating hex labels, and floating central islands.
The deferred section lists later additive genres and components such as timeline-cascade, mesh-core vs radial-hub, person-portrait, logo-wall refinement, and generated golden-hour imagery.
The execution section calls for parallel builder agents to create v2.html files, render v2 PNGs, update the manifest, then commit and tag pass-2.
standouts: The global rules establish a precise warm architectural visual language with explicit hex colors, proportions, opacity ranges, and role-based accent semantics.
The file distinguishes immediate v2 convergence work from deferred additive depth.
var-15 is explicitly marked as a critical correction because a gold-filled solution plate violates the stated invariant that gold is never a fill/background.
conventions: Uses numbered global rules with compact G1-G10 identifiers.
Uses per-mockup bullet entries keyed by mockup ids such as var-1-trizone and var-15-currentsolution.
Uses imperative design language with prohibitions, target proportions, color hexes, opacity ranges, and rendering instructions.
Uses shorthand G* to mean applying the relevant global rules to a mockup.
Keeps the brief in Markdown with section headings and bold emphasis for invariant-level constraints.
concerns: Some instructions rely on subjective visual judgment, including phrases like "get the language & look RIGHT", "too timid", "good", and "reads as a gimmick".
The execution workflow references a manifest, v1 files, renders, sources, and gallery compare behavior without defining their exact paths or schema in this file.
test-A/test-B/test-C are given an unresolved branch: bring to G* as v2 or mark legacy.
The file says generated imagery is the eventual fix for var-3 but also treats the current light pool as a stand-in, leaving the replacement boundary open.
notes: This is a detailed v2 visual correction brief for aligning a set of mockups around a warm architectural slide language.
questions: What exact manifest format should receive the v2 entries and changes lists?
Which source file is meant by "the source" for each mockup?
Should test-A/test-B/test-C be updated to v2 or marked legacy?
What render command or renderer settings are expected for renders/<id>.v2.png?
When should the generated imagery deferred for var-3 and the warm pole be introduced?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the content: it presents a v2 fix brief for mockups, then enumerates global rules, per-mockup edits, deferred items, and execution steps.
apparent_themes: Separation of immediate fixes from deferred depth
intention_signals: The file appears to be trying to turn critique findings into an actionable production pass that standardizes visual language while preserving per-mockup corrections.
novelty: The brief combines design critique, exact color/opacity/proportion constraints, per-mockup remediation notes, and build/render/versioning instructions in one operational Markdown ledger.
contribution: Contributes a file-local rule set for warm-metal slide aesthetics, specific remediation notes for each named mockup, and a workflow for generating and recording v2 artifacts without introducing a shared token system yet.
summary_for_embedding: A Markdown v2 fix brief for counterpart-design mockups defining global visual rules, per-mockup corrections, deferred design work, and execution steps for v2 HTML, renders, manifest entries, commit, and tag.
intention_for_embedding: It was written to guide a coordinated mockup update pass after critique, aligning layouts, colors, surfaces, titles, texture, footers, and artifact production before later tokenisation.
